title: narcissus × incomparabilis var. aurantius
intro: Narcissus × incomparabilis var. aurantius, also known as the Golden Narcissus or Daffodil, is a hybrid of the Narcissus pseudonarcissus and Narcissus pseudonarcissus var. cantabricus, characterized by its unique bright yellow trumpet-shaped flowers. The Golden Narcissus has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, glycosides, and essential oils. The health benefits of the Golden Narcissus include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as a treatment for digestive problems and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the Golden Narcissus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
